steps.title: Identify the problem-solution dynamic
steps.content: The passage describes a research limitation caused by limited data, then shifts to show researchers acquiring new data. The blank asks what they did to the shortcoming after this breakthrough. The key signal: new data directly addresses the root cause of the limitation.

steps.title: Match the verb to the improvement
steps.content: Mitigate means to reduce or lessen something harmful — which is exactly what new data does to the previous limitation on research scope. Presuppose contradicts the timeline of gaining data, validate applies to evidence rather than shortcomings, and categorize is unrelated to addressing limitations. This matches choice A — A is correct.
